name: ExtensionRelatedArtifact_Type
title: R5: documentation | justification | citation | predecessor | successor | derived-from | depends-on | composed-of | part-of | amends | amended-with | appends | appended-with | cites | cited-by | comments-on | comment-in | contains | contained-in | corrects | correction-in | replaces | replaced-with | retracts | retracted-by | signs | similar-to | supports | supported-with | transforms | transformed-into | transformed-with | documents | specification-of | created-with | cite-as
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `RelatedArtifact.type` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`RelatedArtifact.type` 1..1 `code`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `RelatedArtifact.type` 1..1 `code`
*  R4B: `RelatedArtifact.type` 1..1 `code`
*  R4: `RelatedArtifact.type` 1..1 `code`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `RelatedArtifact.type` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `RelatedArtifact.type` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

element.short: R5: documentation | justification | citation | predecessor | successor | derived-from | depends-on | composed-of | part-of | amends | amended-with | appends | appended-with | cites | cited-by | comments-on | comment-in | contains | contained-in | corrects | correction-in | replaces | replaced-with | retracts | retracted-by | signs | similar-to | supports | supported-with | transforms | transformed-into | transformed-with | documents | specification-of | created-with | cite-as
element.comment:
Element `RelatedArtifact.type` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `RelatedArtifact.type` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The presence of both sides of a relationship type (e.g. successor and predecessor) is required to support use cases where one side of a relationship is not represented in FHIR. However, this feature SHALL NOT be used to create bi-directional resource links in FHIR instances. Specifically, following the methodology of "new points to old" and "many points to one", when using related artifact elements to describe and reference FHIR resources, the type element SHALL be drawn from the fhir-related-artifact-type ValueSet.
